definition: Total fertility rates across OECD nations have fallen below the 2.1 replacement level and show no historical precedent for recovery once below 1.5. This is a near-universal, effectively irreversible demographic condition with ultra-low outliers like South Korea (0.72) and China (~1.0-1.1) showing no floor.
evidence: 0/25 recoveries from TFR <1.5 back to replacement
UN median variant overestimates by ~0.3 children/woman (p<0.01) across 50 countries
Pronatalist policy recovery base rate ~16%, and recovery means ~1.6 not replacement
open_questions: Is there a biological or social floor below which TFR cannot fall?
Can any pronatalist policy achieve sustained recovery to replacement level?
